name: sbom-syft
description: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) generation using Syft for container images, filesystems, and archives. Detects packages across 28+ ecosystems with multi-format output support (CycloneDX, SPDX, syft-json). Enables vulnerability assessment, license compliance, and supply chain security. Use when: (1) Generating SBOMs for container images or applications, (2) Analyzing software dependencies and packages for vulnerability scanning, (3) Tracking license compliance across dependencies, (4) Integrating SBOM generation into CI/CD for supply chain security, (5) Creating signed SBOM attestations for software provenance.
capabilities: Generate SBOMs for container images in CycloneDX, SPDX, and Syft formats
Scan filesystems and source code directories for package dependencies
Create CI/CD pipeline integrations for automated SBOM generation
Analyze SBOM output for vulnerabilities using tools like Grype
Configure signed SBOM attestations for software provenance verification
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing SBOM generation guidance using Syft CLI tool. No executable code exists in this skill. Static scanner flagged patterns within markdown documentation and YAML templates containing example commands, CI/CD workflows, and security rule templates. All findings are false positives since this skill only contains documentation and templates for legitimate supply chain security workflows.
valueStatement: Software supply chain attacks are increasing. Organizations need visibility into their software dependencies. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for generating comprehensive SBOMs using Syft, enabling vulnerability tracking, license compliance, and secure software provenance.
userTitle: Generate Software Bill of Materials with Syft
limitations: Requires Syft CLI tool installation or Docker access for container scanning
Does not perform vulnerability scanning itself, only generates SBOM for downstream tools
Limited to 28+ supported ecosystems, may not detect custom or niche package managers
Cannot scan encrypted or password-protected container images without proper credentials

useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
useCases.description: Automate SBOM generation in build pipelines for every container release

useCases.title: Vulnerability Tracking
useCases.description: Generate SBOMs to identify all components in container images for scanners

useCases.title: License Compliance
useCases.description: Extract and track license information across all dependencies for compliance

promptTemplates.title: Basic Container SBOM
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a CycloneDX SBOM for the nginx:latest container image using Syft

promptTemplates.title: Filesystem Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan the /app directory and generate an SPDX-formatted SBOM for Python dependencies

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Format Output
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate SBOMs in all three formats (CycloneDX, SPDX, Syft JSON) for myapp:v2.0

promptTemplates.title: Pipeline Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds, generates an SBOM, and scans with Grype

bestPractices: Generate SBOMs at build time and store them alongside container images for traceability
Use signed attestations with cosign to verify SBOM integrity and prevent tampering
Integrate SBOM generation into CI/CD to ensure every release has complete software provenance
antiPatterns: Generating SBOMs only after a security incident instead of proactively at build time
Storing SBOMs in insecure locations where they could be modified by attackers
Using only one SBOM format when your pipeline tools support multiple formats

faq.question: What SBOM formats does Syft support?
faq.answer: Syft supports CycloneDX JSON/XML, SPDX JSON/tag-value, Syft JSON, GitHub JSON, and custom templates.

faq.question: What ecosystems can Syft analyze?
faq.answer: Syft supports 28+ ecosystems including Python, JavaScript, Go, Java, Ruby, Rust, PHP, .NET, and more.

faq.question: Can Syft scan running containers?
faq.answer: No, Syft analyzes images or filesystems. Use docker save or scan directory contents for running containers.

faq.question: Is SBOM data safe to share?
faq.answer: SBOMs contain package names and versions but may expose internal details. Share only with trusted parties.

faq.question: Why is my SBOM missing packages?
faq.answer: Try scanning with --scope all-layers or verify package manager files exist in the image.

faq.question: How does Syft compare to Trivy?
faq.answer: Syft generates SBOMs while Trivy scans for vulnerabilities. They work well together: Syft creates SBOM, Trivy scans.
